description_long: This is a detailed model of a NASA Space Shuttle orbiter, featuring a white fuselage with black thermal protection tiles along the nose, wing edges, and underbelly. The wings are delta-shaped and low-mounted, with a slight upward dihedral and no visible winglets. The rear of the shuttle displays three large main engines flanked by two smaller orbital maneuvering system (OMS) pods. The tail section includes a single tall vertical stabilizer with a rudder and a split speed brake. The cockpit is located at the forward end, with multiple windows arranged in a wraparound configuration. Markings include the NASA logo, United States flag, and other mission insignia.
description: A NASA Space Shuttle orbiter with a white and black color scheme, delta wings, three main engines, and a forward-mounted cockpit with multiple windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the broad delta wings extending outward.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the NASA and United States markings, and the vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the three main engines, OMS pods, and the full span of the delta wings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the fuselage, wing, and tail configuration with visible markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-mounted delta wings with slight upward dihedral, no winglets, and straight trailing edges.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted rocket engines with two additional OMS pods flanking the main engines.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single tall vertical stabilizer and integrated rudder; no horizontal stabilizers visible.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with one nose gear and two main gear units (not visible in deployed state).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with a wraparound window configuration.
